prompt: WebSocket connections are dropping after exactly 60 seconds of inactivity

turns.content:
<task>
WebSocket connections are dropping after exactly 60 seconds of inactivity. Users lose their chat connection.
</task>

1	 import asyncio
2	 import websockets
3	 from ws.manager import ConnectionManager
4	 
5	 manager = ConnectionManager()
6	 
7	 async def handle_connection(websocket, path):
8	     user_id = await authenticate(websocket)
9	     manager.connect(user_id, websocket)
10	     try:
11	         async for message in websocket:

13	     finally:
14	         manager.disconnect(user_id)

arguments.reasoning: The 60-second timeout is a classic nginx issue. Nginx has a default proxy_read_timeout of 60 seconds. If no data is sent on the WebSocket for 60 seconds, nginx closes the connection. Two fixes needed: 1) Set proxy_read_timeout to something much higher in nginx, 2) Implement a ping/pong keepalive in the WebSocket handler to prevent idle timeouts.

arguments.query: nginx websocket proxy_read_timeout keepalive ping pong

turns.content:
1. NGINX WebSocket Proxying - https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/websocket.html
   By default, connection will be closed if proxied server does not transmit any data within 60 seconds. Use proxy_read_timeout to increase.
2. WebSocket Keepalive with NGINX - https://www.nginx.com/blog/websocket-nginx/
   Set proxy_read_timeout 3600s; and implement application-level ping/pong.

arguments.title: Nginx WebSocket timeout fix
arguments.content: Nginx default proxy_read_timeout is 60s. For WebSocket, set proxy_read_timeout to 3600s or higher. Also implement application-level ping/pong (every 30s) as defense against other proxies/load balancers.

turns.content: Finding recorded: Nginx WebSocket timeout fix

arguments.message:
Found the cause: nginx's default `proxy_read_timeout` is 60 seconds, which matches the symptom exactly. When no data flows through the WebSocket for 60 seconds, nginx closes it.

I'll fix both sides:
1. Increase `proxy_read_timeout` in nginx.conf
2. Add a ping/pong heartbeat in the WebSocket handler (defense in depth against other proxies)

arguments.summary: Root cause: nginx proxy_read_timeout defaults to 60s, closing idle WebSocket connections. Will fix nginx config and add application-level keepalive.

next_steps.description: Add proxy_read_timeout and proxy_send_timeout to nginx.conf WebSocket location
Add ping/pong heartbeat to ws/handler.py every 30 seconds

arguments.content:
import asyncio
import websockets
from ws.manager import ConnectionManager

manager = ConnectionManager()
PING_INTERVAL = 30

async def handle_connection(websocket, path):
    user_id = await authenticate(websocket)
    manager.connect(user_id, websocket)
    try:
        async for message in websocket:
            await manager.broadcast(user_id, message)
    finally:
        manager.disconnect(user_id)

async def start_server():
    async with websockets.serve(
        handle_connection, '0.0.0.0', 8765,
        ping_interval=PING_INTERVAL,
        ping_timeout=10
    ):
        await asyncio.Future()

arguments.summary: Fixed WebSocket timeout: increased nginx proxy_read_timeout to 3600s and added websockets library ping/pong with 30s interval and 10s timeout.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed the 60-second WebSocket disconnect. Root cause was nginx's default proxy_read_timeout. Set to 3600s and added application-level ping/pong keepalive (30s interval). Reload nginx after deploying.
